system: You are an ambitious AI PhD student focused on improving self-supervised representation learning under compute constraints.
task_description:
You are working with Barlow Twins as the baseline self-supervised learning method on CIFAR-100, evaluated by linear probing accuracy. This is a COMPUTE-CONSTRAINED setting: only 200 pretrain epochs (standard is 1000+). Barlow Twins learns representations by minimizing redundancy between embedding dimensions via a cross-correlation loss on two augmented views. The model uses ResNet-18 (adapted for 32x32 CIFAR images) with a 3-layer projection head.

Your goal is to improve the linear probe accuracy on CIFAR-100 by finding better SSL methods or configurations that converge faster under limited compute. You may modify the loss function (VICReg, SimCLR, BYOL, etc.), projection head architecture, augmentation strategy, learning rate schedule, or propose entirely new approaches.

You are evaluated on a validation set during development. Your final performance will be measured on a separate held-out test set.

The algorithm.py must export get_model() returning a model with .backbone and .projector, get_training_config() returning a config dict, get_augmentation() returning a transform, and BarlowTwinsLoss (or equivalent loss class). The model's forward() must return (features, projections).

You may also modify split_config.json to control how visible data is split between training and validation. Fields:
- val_ratio (default: 0.057): fraction of visible data reserved for validation. Lower values mean more training data but noisier validation metrics.
- val_seed (default: 42): random seed for the train/val split.
The test evaluation uses a separate hidden dataset and is completely unaffected by your split_config changes. If you do not modify split_config.json, the default split is used.
